sourceMovie.title: Hokum
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hokum (2026) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Damian McCarthy's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Shining
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hokum worked for you, The Shining (1980) is a strong next pick. The two films share horror throughlines and unease that lingers after the credits, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hokum?
faq.answer: Try The Shining, Gremlins, The Devil's Advocate—each shares DNA with Hokum while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hokum?
faq.answer: Hokum (2026) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hokum worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
